Name: Sensaphone Sentinel Monitoring System with Cellular Modem
Description: Cloud-Based Monitoring... Without Network Connections A supervised internet connection assures the Sentinel is online and monitoring at all times Store your sensor readings in the cloud to automatically protect against data loss and provide limitless information storage Receive alarm notifications through a cellular connection Notifies an unlimited number of people by e-mail, text message or voice phone calls in the event of an alarm Up to twelve external sensors monitor temperature, humidity, water and more A cellular modem provides operation in locations where an ethernet connection is unobtainable  Temperature sensing range of -109° to 168°F | -85° to 76°C Manage multiple units, make programming changes and access status conditions using a simple, powerful web-based interface Included external antenna allows Sentinel to be placed in areas where it may not otherwise get cellular coverage, such as inside a building or steel box  Subscription costs ($24.95 per month) includes all online hosting, cellular connection fees, and unlimited text, phone and email alerts Contact us before purchasing to confirm cellular coverage Cellular modem and SIM card included Made in USA The Sentinel Monitoring System with Cellular Modem offers an easy-to-install, easy-to-configure monitoring solution for locations with no access to a landline or Internet connection. The Sentinel system monitors up to 12 different environmental and equipment status conditions including temperature, humidity, power failure and water detection. When the system identifies issues, it instantly sends alerts via phone, text or email over a cellular connection. It can also deliver daily event reports, and provide audit logs. Users can access information and make system changes from any web-enabled device. The system stores all readings in the cloud, which protects against data loss, provides unlimited information storage and allows multiple devices to be managed from one account. No software is required, so installation, integration and management are easy. INCLUDED: Internal rechargeable battery-backup system will provide 8 hours of continuous monitoring and alerting in the event of a power outage. Name: 2.8k Type Room Temperature Sensor
Description: Easy temperature monitoring Measures temperature from 0° to 120°F | -17° to 49°C Requires additional accessory wire to connect Sensaphone For use in clean, indoor environments The Room Temperature Sensor can be used with most Sensaphone models to measure the temperature in a clean dry environment. The Sensaphone will trigger an alarm when the temperature falls outside of the programmed high or low temperature limit. The sensor is mounted on a flat surface anywhere ambient air temperatures need to be measured and connects to the Sensaphone with optional accessory wire. This model is recommended to be located in clean, dry environment. For outdoor use model FGD-0101 is recommended. Name: Contact Type Magnetic Reed Door and Window Switch
Description: Detect Unauthorized Entry or Intrusion Compatible with all Sensaphone models Sends alarm signal to Sensaphone when the switch opens or closes Several Magnetic Reed Switches may be installed in series on a single continuous circuit provided all switches will be normally closed at the same time The magnetic reed switch may be used with all Sensaphone Models to detect any unauthorized entry or intrusion. They are usually installed on doors or windows to detect their opening and closing. Several magnetic reed switches may be wired in series to one Sensaphone input. The switch is usually installed to function in a normally closed position when the door or window is closed. The complete Magnetic Reed Switch assembly comes in two main parts: a magnet in a plastic housing, and a switch with screw terminals in another, separate plastic housing. Name: 2.8K Type Weatherproof Temperature Sensor
Description: Weatherproof temperature monitoring Measures temperature from -20° to 176°F | -28° to 80°C Accurate to ± 1°F |  ± .56°C Includes 12’ of wire and mounting clip Suitable for use in wet or dirty environments The Weatherproof Temperature Sensor can be used with all Sensaphone models to measure the temperature in a wet or dirty environment. The Sensaphone will trigger an alarm when the temperature falls outside of the programmed high or low temperature limit. The sensor can be anchored in place using the included mounting clip. The sensor is ideal for cold storage applications such as refrigerators or freezers, agricultural applications such as greenhouses or anywhere a temperature measurement is required away from the Sensaphone. The included 12 feet of wire can be extended up to 1,000’ with optional accessory wire. An optional NIST traceable certificate is available. Name: Ethernet-to-WiFi Adapter Accessory
Description: Add connectivity to any network based Sensaphone Connect any network based Sensaphone to WiFi Weatherproof housing allows unit to be mounted outdoors Users program the adapter to connect with a WiFi network via downloadable software The distance between the adapter and router is limited by the strength of the WiFi network, however, much farther distances can be achieved by using another adapter to strengthen the WiFi signal Users program the adapter to connect with a WiFi network via downloadable software The Ethernet-to-WiFi Adapter can be used with all wired network based Sensaphone models to provide WiFi access. The adapter allows you to place your Sensaphone in a location where the required Ethernet connection may not be available, but a WiFi signal is available. The unit features an IP65-Rated weatherproof housing that ensures continued operation in harsh outdoor environments or in climates of extreme cold, heat, or continuous moisture. The kit also contains a power supply and pole or wall mounting hardware. It is connected to the Sensaphone remote monitoring system via an Ethernet cable (not included). Description: A supervised internet connection assures the Stratus EMS is online and monitoring at all times Store your sensor readings in the cloud to automatically protect against data loss and provide limitless information storage Notifies an unlimited number of people by e-mail, text message or voice phone calls in the event of an alarm Poll Modbus registers from other equipment via RS485 and TCP/IP 12 universal digital or analog inputs with available 24VDC (50mA) per zone to monitor temperature, humidity, water and more Temperature sensing range of -109° to 168°F | -85° to 76°C Manage multiple units, make programming changes and access status conditions using a simple, powerful web-based interface Fits in a 1U rack space Up to 30 hours of extended battery backup life 2 relay outputs for manual or automatic switching API Data Interchange Formats: JSON, HTTPS, POST, REST web services, cURL Ability to use Sensaphone WEB600 and WSG30 as expansion modules Sensaphone’s cloud-based Stratus EMS allows you to monitor data center facilities and environments with the same degree of certainty you’ve come to expect from Sensaphone. Users can check critical conditions of their climate and equipment-sensitive commodities at any time. The Stratus EMS monitors, delivers alarms, and data logs 64 input/output points from Modbus inputs in addition to 12 universal digital or analog inputs. It supports Modbus RTU/485 and Modbus TCP which make is capable of reading data from many IDF, PDU, UPS and Building Automation Systems. It takes the burden out of managing your system by giving you access to your readings from anywhere using a simple, powerful web-based interface. If there’s a disruption, you’ll be the first to know. Alerts can be sent straight to your mobile device—keeping you updated and giving you peace-of-mind wherever you are so you can remain focused on business as usual. INCLUDED: Internal rechargeable battery-backup system will provide up to 30 hours of continuous monitoring and alerting in the event of a power outage. Name: Contact Type Spot Water Detection Sensor

Name: Contact Type Temp Alert Temperature Switch
Description: Monitor air temperature from 20° to 110°F | 6.7° to 43.3°C Monitors temperatures from 20° to 110°F | 6.7° to 43.3°C Sends a simple alarm or no alarm status to the Sensaphone Provides a local display of temperature reading Multiple sensors can be added to one Sensaphone input Separate output for high and low alarm signals No power required to operate 1 year warranty The Temp Alert Temperature Switch can be used with all Sensaphone models. It monitors air temperature from 20° to 110°F | 6.7° to 43.3°C. By adjusting the temperature limit arms, you can select the acceptable temperature limits for your application. Simply use a screwdriver to set the temperature limit arms on the rear of the device. When the ambient air temperature rises or falls to the limit arms, the temperature sensor triggers an alarm. The temperature alert can be mounted to any wall or vertical surface. Multiple temp alerts may be wired in parallel to a single input. It requires no power for operation and mounts easily on any flat surface. Name: Contact Type Humidistat Humidity Switch
Description: Detect high or low humidity Monitors humidity ranges from 20% to 80% Alarm values are set on the sensor Sensor provides simple alarm or no alarm status to Sensaphone Receive alerts from your Sensaphone when the status changes The Humidistat Humidity Switch is a hard-wired humidity sensor that can be used with all Sensaphone models to detect high or low humidity levels. Users set a high or low alarm limit by adjusting the dial on the front of the sensor. When that limit is exceeded, the device will trip an alarm to the Sensaphone. Made from high impact white plastic, a nylon sensing element provides long life and accurate humidity control. The sensor has a range of 20% to 80% relative humidity. Multiple sensors may be wired to a single input. The humidistat switch can be installed in any location, home or office, and will work accurately for many years. Name: 4-20mA Type Duct Mount Air Flow Transmitter
Description: Monitor air flow in duct work Measures air flow from 0-8m/s or 0-16m/s Accurate to ±5% Set high and low limits by programming the Sensaphone Sensaphone will go into alarm when air flow level exceeds limit Compensates for changes in air temperature Telescopic arm allows sensor to be placed at correct depth into duct Requires 24VDC power supply (Part # FGD-0070) The Sensaphone Air Flow Sensor will measure and provide real time air flow values to any Sensaphone that accepts a 4-20mA signal. When the air flow level falls outside of the Sensaphone’s programmed high or low limit, the user will receive an alarm which displays or reads the current air flow value. The sensor registers the air flow speed according to a thermal principal based on the fact that the cooling action of air increases with airspeed. The duct mount enclosure allows the sensor to be placed inside an air duct. A 24V DC power supply is required to power the sensor. Name: 4-20mA Type Split Core Current Sensor
Description: Measure current flowing to equipment Measures current from 0 to 200A Set high or low limit by programming the Sensaphone Sensaphone will go into alarm when current exceeds limit Available in 50 Amp or 200 Amp options The Sensaphone Split Core Current sensor will measure and provide real time current values to any Sensaphone that accepts a 4-20mA signal. Name: 4-20mA Type Soil Moisture Sensor
Description: Research grade accuracy Measures soil moisture from 0 to 100% VWC Uses a 70 MHz frequency, which minimizes salinity and textural effects, making the MAS-1 accurate in almost any soil or soil-less media Factory calibrations are included for mineral soils, potting soils, and rockwool Set high and low limits by programming the Sensaphone Sensaphone will go into alarm when soil moisture level exceeds limit Delivers research-grade accuracy The Sensaphone Soil Moisture Sensor will measure and provide real time moisture values to any Sensaphone that accepts a 4-20mA signal. When the soil moisture level falls outside of the Sensaphone’s programmed high or low limit, the user will receive an alarm which displays or reads the current moisture value. The sensor determines volumetric water content (VWC) by measuring the dielectric constant of the media using capacitance/frequency domain technology. It minimizes salinity and textural effects, making it accurate in almost any soil or soil-less media. Factory calibrations are included for mineral soils, potting soils, and rockwool. A 24V DC power supply is required to power the sensor (Part # FGD-0070). Name: 2.8K Type Temperature Sensor in Glass Bead Vial
Description: Buffer momentary temperature fluctuations Measures temperature from -20° to 115°F | -28° to 46°C Accurate to ± 1°F |  ± .56°C Glass bead filled vial will buffer any momentary fluctuations that may cause a preliminary alarm Includes 12’ of wire Suitable for use in wet or dirty environments The Temperature Sensor in Glass Bead Vial can be used with most Sensaphone models to measure the temperature of objects in a refrigerator or freezer. By placing the sensor in a bottle filled with glass beads users will receive a reading that represents the temperature of the refrigerator’s contents rather than the ambient air temperature. The Sensaphone will trigger an alarm when the temperature falls outside of the programmed high or low temperature limit.  The glass bead filled vial will also buffer any momentary fluctuations that may cause a preliminary alarm dial out, such as defrost cycles, opening of doors, and fans circulating air. The glass beads will slow the response time of the sensor allowing normal daily operations to occur without having an alarm. Name: 2.8K Type Temperature Sensor Display
Description: Add a local display to any 2.8k Type Temperature Sensor Connects to any Sensaphone 2.8k type temperature sensor to provide a local temperature reading Battery powered for up to one year Logs and displays high and low temperature Audible alarm Relay output can be programmed to turn on light or siren Optional power supply available The Local Temperature Display can be used with all Sensaphone 2.8k type temperature sensors. This provides an at-a-glance reading of the current temperature as well as the minimum and maximum temperatures. The display is ideal for any location where onsite staff need a quick, convenient visual reading of the sensor, saving them the time of checking the monitoring system's data. The Local Temperature Display also: Logs and displays the highest and lowest temperatures Provides an audible alarm for out-of-range temperatures Offers a relay output that can be programmed to turn on a light or siren for additional onsite alarm notifications. When the temperature falls out of the programmed high and low limits, the display triggers an audible alarm, immediately alerting onsite staff of a potential problem. This alarm is in addition to the alerts that the Sensaphone monitoring system sends to designated personnel when temperatures fall out of range. The temperature sensor display is easy to program by using the four buttons on the front. It connects to the sensor and to most Sensaphone monitoring devices via screw terminals on the bottom. The display comes with a battery that powers it for up to one year, but you can also use an optional external power supply. Name: 2.8k Type Temperature Sensor in Glycol Vial
Description: Buffer momentary temperature fluctuations Measures temperature from -20° to 176°F | -28° to 80°C Accurate to ± 1°F |  ± .56°C Glycol filled vial will buffer any momentary fluctuations that may cause a preliminary alarm Includes 12’ of wire The Temperature Sensor in Glycol Vial can be used with most Sensaphone models to measure the temperature of objects in a refrigerator or freezer. By placing the sensor in a bottle filled with glycol, users will receive a reading that represents the temperature of the refrigerator’s contents rather than the ambient air temperature. The Sensaphone will trigger an alarm when the temperature falls outside of the programmed high or low temperature limit. The glycol filled vial will also buffer any momentary fluctuations that may cause a preliminary alarm dial out, such as defrost cycles, the opening of doors, and fans circulating air. The glycol will slow the response time of the sensor, allowing normal daily operations to occur without having an alarm. The included 12 feet of wire can be extended with optional accessory wire. Description: Receive alarms and datalog pressure values Measures pressure from 0 to 200 psi, or 0-1,000 psi Requires a 24VDC power supply FGD-0070 Compatible with refrigerants Suitable for higher range pressure applications, such as steam boilers and the high side/discharge lines on HVAC equipment which can exceed 500PSI The Sensaphone Pressure Sensor will measure and provide real time pressure values to any Sensaphone that accepts a 4-20mA signal. When the pressure level falls outside of the Sensaphone’s programmed high or low limit, the user will receive an alarm which displays or reads the current pressure value. An alarm from the pressure sensor can indicate a problem with a pump, a possible clogged influent line, loss of suction, a downstream clog or perhaps a burst pipe. Any of those failures can cause a pump motor to overheat and warp. A 24V DC power supply is required to power the sensor. Name: 4-20mA Type Vibration Sensor
Description: Monitor the overall vibration of equipment Measures vibrations from 0 to 25 RMS Requires a 24VDC power supply (FGD-0070) The vibration sensor comes with two threaded adapters allowing it to screw into either: (a) 1/4"-28 UNF threaded hole, min. depth 13mm  or  (b) M8 threaded hole, min. depth 10mm The Sensaphone Vibration Sensor will measure and provide real time vibration values to any Sensaphone that accepts a 4-20mA signal. When the vibration level falls outside of the Sensaphone’s programmed high or low limit, the user will receive an alarm which displays or reads the current vibration value. Vibration on industrial equipment can be continuously monitored for unusual operating conditions and potential failure. The vibration sensor is designed to be permanently mounted on industrial equipment and continuously monitor the changes in a machine's vibration level. Detecting these changes early on prevents machinery from catastrophic failure, secondary damage, and expensive down time. The sensor comes with a 5m cable and is available with an optional magnetic mounting base. A 24V DC power supply is required to power the sensor. Name: Dissolved Oxygen Transducer
Description: Product Description The AM-ODO-TX is one of the most compact, durable, and user friendly dissolved oxygen sensor on the market. It is an optical sensor that utilizes fluorescence quenching technology. The AM-ODO-TX is part of the AquaMetrix smart sensor series. The probes in this series are direct output digital sensors with the precision of a controller and the accuracy of our core probes. The AM-ODO-TX can communicate via RS485 or Modbus protocols. This innovative sensor outputs two (2) digital-based analog (4-20 mA) signals that can be set as 2 of 3 options: Percent-saturation, concentration, or temperature. It can connect to the AquaMetrix AM-2300 controller, AM-2252 controller or directly to a PLC. Optical DO sensors are inherently self-referencing, require no warm-up period, do not require constant flow to maintain a stable reading, and are less affected by fouling. The ODO-TX is reliable and reads accurately within 90 seconds of going from dry to wet conditions. The reliability is backed by the factory-programmed 90-point calibration, stored directly on in a memory chip embedded in the membrane cap. The sensor membrane lasts for one to two years and is inexpensive to replace. The intuitive membrane cap Visual Lifecycle Indication (VLI) methodology lets users know when to replace the cap, giving the user comfort in reliable operation. New membrane caps come pre-calibrated, so for maintenance simply install the new cap, and you’re ready to go! When needed, intermittent calibration is simple and can be done on a controller or directly on the probe.  This sensor is proven via extensive testing and its core technology helped develop the EPA-approved method that other manufacturers follow. Technical Specifications: Measuring Range: 0 to 60ppm ( 0 to 600% saturation)Accuracy at 25 0C DO (0-20 mg/L), (20-60 mg/L) :±0.1 mg/L ±2 %Temperature: ± 0.1 °C Resolution DO: 10 ppb (0.01 ppm) Temp: 0.01 °C Response Time: T90 < 45 seconds, T95 < 60 seconds Operation Temperature: -5 to 50 °C Depth: 210 m (689 ft) @ 25°C Maximum Pressure: 150 psi from 0° to 50°C

Name: VFC Refrigerator Temperature Sensor with Display
Description: VFC Refrigerator Temperature Sensor with Display Continually monitors temperatures Clearly displays the temperature  Displays high and low temperatures Provides an audible alarm for out-of-range temperatures Can be programmed to turn on a light or siren for additional onsite alarm notifications Made in USA The Temperature Sensor Display helps meet compliance when monitoring vaccines for the CDC’s Vaccines For Children (VFC) program. From the factory, the display comes with a NIST certified temperature probe in a glass bead buffered bottle for installation inside of the refrigerator. The display is mounted to the outside of the refrigerator and then connected to a Sensaphone system that is capable of data logging. Users can read the refrigerator’s temperature on a 2” LCD display while the device passes the data along to the Sensaphone. Data can be viewed remotely, data logged, and monitored for high and low-temperature alarms. The Local Temperature Display also: Displays the highest and lowest temperatures Provides an audible alarm for out-of-range temperatures Can turn on a light or siren for additional onsite alarm notifications When the temperature falls out of the programmed high and low limits, the display triggers an audible alarm, immediately alerting onsite staff of a potential problem. This alarm is in addition to the alerts that the Sensaphone monitoring system sends to designated personnel when temperatures fall out of range. The display comes with a plug-in power supply and a battery that powers the device in the event of a power failure. Page Title: Sensaphone IMS-4000E Enterprise Monitoring Node Expansion Unit
META Description: The IMS-4000E Node allows IMS-4000 users to expand their infrastructure and monitor additional inputs in locations away from the IMS-4000E Host. Up to thirty-one Nodes can be used with each IMS-4000E Host. Much like the Host, the Node can monitor eight inputs for environmental conditions and or alarm contacts from other computer equipment such as UPS systems. The Node connects to the Host via a RJ-45 10/100BASE-T Ethernet and is monitored via the ConsoleView software included with the Host.

Name: IMS-4000 Dual Relay Output Module
Description: Control two devices from any IMS-4000E Host or Node The module connects to an IMS-4000 Host or Node to receive its control instructions. Connects to IMS-4000E Host or Node using included using the 9-pin null modem cable The IMS-4310E Dual Relay Output Module allows you to control two devices from any IMS-4000E Host or Node. Each output may be controlled either manually or automatically. Each output has the option of providing either a dry relay contact or 12VDC power. Two switches located inside the device select the output type. The IMS-4000E allows you to program an Alarm Response whenever an environmental or IP Alarm occurs. An Alarm Response is an action that occurs automatically whenever an alarm occurs. Using the Relay Module you can automatically turn On, Off, or Cycle equipment whenever an Environmental or IP alarm occurs.

Name: IMS-4000 Node w/ Dry Contact Inputs
Description: Monitor Dry Contacts with your IMS-4000E system Allows users to monitor up to 8 dry contact inputs   Monitor equipment such as alarm panels, UPS alarms, PDU alarms, automatic transfer switches or security systems Works in conjunction with Sensaphone IMS-4000 Host Battery backup in the event of a power failure Can be set to alarm on power failure The IMS-4000 Dry Contact Node provides eight dry contact inputs (normally open/normally closed) and works in conjunction with the IMS Host. When the contact switches position the Expansion Node will communicate the change back to the IMS system and users will receive an alarm via phone, text message or email. It is ideal for monitoring the alarm outputs of devices such as a UPS, power distribution unit, fire alarm panel, automatic transfer switch or security system. The device communicates with the IMS-4000E Host via its Ethernet connection. The unit uses four AA rechargeable NiMH batteries (included) for power backup in the event of a power failure. Both main power and battery charge are monitored and can be programmed to trip alarms as well. The unit comes in a black plastic enclosure with tabs for wall mounting. Rubber feet are also included for placement on a shelf/desktop. Name: IMS Solution Ultra Low Temperature Sensor
Description: Monitor temperatures as low as -85°C Optional glass bead vial will buffer any momentary fluctuations that may cause a preliminary alarm dial out, such as defrost cycles, opening of doors, and fans circulating air Measures temperatures from -121° to 53°F | -86° to 12°C Available with NIST certification The Ultra Low Temperature Sensors are designed to connect to the Sensaphone IMS product line and monitor temperatures in refrigerators or freezers. The electrical connection between the sensor and IMS unit is made via the attached RJ-45 patch cable. The sensor probe may be secured inside equipment using the included clamp or it may be hung using tie wraps. Do not install the black plastic enclosure inside of a refrigerator or freezer. This sensor includes a remote temperature probe so that temperature measurements can be made in a freezer or other harsh environments. The sensor is available with an optional glass bead vial which helps to prevent false temperature readings when a refrigerator or freezer door is opened or goes into a defrost cycle. In addition they are available with an optional NIST temperature certification. The Ultra Low Temperature Sensors come with a 7′ cable with an RJ-45 plug on the end and a weatherproof probe with 12′ of cable. If the sensor installation is close to the IMS unit, you can plug it directly into an IMS environmental input. If the cable is too short you can use the included RJ-45 adaptor and a longer patch cable to extend the length. For applications where the sensor may be far from the IMS unit, you may utilize your existing network wiring infrastructure. For example, the sensor may be installed in another room or another floor. Connect the sensor to your structured wiring network via an RJ-45 jack. At the other end, connect a patch cable from your structured wiring patch panel directly to the Host or Node environment input.  Technical Specifications Sensor Temperature Range: -122° to 53°F | -86° to 12°CAccuracy: 3°F | 1.7°C  Connection: RJ45Housing: PlasticDimensions: 2.1 x 1.3 x .6" | 53 x 33 x 15mmSensor Series: IMS SolutionNotes: Available with NIST Certification Downloads IMS Solution Ultra Low Temperature SensorIMS Solution Ultra Low Temperature Sensor in Glass Bead Vial

After mounting the sensor, attach the included 10 of WaterRope, and simply lay its orange cable around your equipment: place it on the floor or beneath a raised floor; inside a dropceiling or in the rafters between floors; or anywhere you must know about water near your equipment. You can attach additional Water Detection sensors to each other to expand your water leak detection capabilities throughout your facility; alternatively, IMS WaterRopes are designed to plug into each other to widen your perimeter for any individual sensor.

Name: IMS Solution 4-20mA Interface
Description: Monitor the output of a 4-20mA transducer Interfaces IMS system with any 4-20mA transducer Set high and low limits of transducer by programming the Sensaphone Sensaphone will go into alarm when transducer level exceeds limit Required RJ45 patch cable powers and connects interface to Sensaphone The IMS 4-20mA Interface is used exclusively with the Sensaphone IMS system system to interface with any third party transducer that outputs a 4-20mA signal. The interface will measure and provide real time values to to the IMS system. When the level falls outside of the IMS’s programmed high or low limit, the user will receive an alarm which displays or reads the current value. The connection from the interface to the IMS system can use an existing CAT5 network wiring infrastructure, which allows it to be installed in locations away from the IMS host device. Name: IMS Solution Magnetic Reed Door and Window Switch
Description: Detect unauthorized entry or intrusion Detects when a door or window is opened or closed Sends Sensaphone into alarm when door or window condition has changed Easily mounted with included hardware Required RJ45 patch cable powers and connects sensor to Sensaphone May be mounted either on a wall or rest on the floor - switch is mounted to doors or windows you wish to monitor. The IMS Magnetic Reed Door and Window Switch is used exclusively with the Sensaphone IMS system system to monitor for any unauthorized entry or intrusion. When the door or window is opened or closed, the sensor will trigger the Sensaphone to go into alarm. The switch is usually installed to function in a normally closed position when the door or window is closed. The complete Magnetic Reed Switch assembly comes in two main parts: a magnet in a plastic housing, and a switch with screw terminals in another, separate plastic housing. It mounts to the door or window with the included hardware. The connection from the sensor to the IMS system can use an existing CAT5 network wiring infrastructure, which powers the sensor and allows it to be installed in locations away from the IMS host device. Name: IMS Solution Infrared Motion Detection Sensor
Description: Monitor for the movement of warm bodies Dual-element passive infrared intrusion detector Reduces false alarms by eliminating background noises and nuisance stimuli Variable pulse-count adjustment, thus making it adaptable for use both inside and outside of server/equipment rooms Compatible with IMS-1000E or IMS-4000E Connects to host device via RJ-45 patch cable The IMS Infrared Motion Detection Sensor is used exclusively with the Sensaphone IMS system system to monitor for the movement of warm bodies in a room. When a person enters the room, the sensor will trigger the Sensaphone to go into alarm. The range is a maximum of 40 feet directly ahead of, and approximately 20 feet to either side of the detector at 40 feet distance. The sensor mounts to the wall with the included hardware. The connection from the sensor to the IMS system can use an existing CAT5 network wiring infrastructure, which powers the sensor and allows it to be installed in locations away from the IMS host device. Name: IMS Solution Air Flow Sensor
Description: Monitor the presence or absence of cool moving air Measures relative air flow passing over the sensor from 0 to 100% Set high and low airflow limits by programming the Sensaphone Sensaphone will go into alarm when air flow level exceeds the high or low limit Easily mounted with included Velcro, double side tape or tie wraps Required RJ45 patch cable powers and connects sensor to Sensaphone The IMS Air Flow Sensor is used exclusively with the Sensaphone IMS system system to measure and provide real time air flow values. Name: 4G/LTE Cell Modem for Sensaphone Cloud Devices
Description: Add Cellular Connectivity to your Sensaphone Cloud Device Ethernet ports for Sensaphone device and Modbus devices (if required) 8-Hour Rechargeable Backup Battery External antenna with 16’ cable Battery Charge Controller to manage battery charging & low voltage disconnect NEMA 4X Enclosure with weatherproof fittings The 4G/LTE Cell Modem for Sensaphone Cloud Devices provides a cellular connection to the internet using AT&T, Verizon or Rogers Wireless (Canada). Name: Sensaphone WSG30 Wireless Temperature Display
Description: The Wireless Temperature Display is used exclusively with the Sensaphone WSG30 connects with all Sensaphone 2.8k and RTD type temperature sensors to provide an at-a-glance reading of the current temperature as well as the minimum and maximum temperatures. The display is ideal for any location where onsite staff need a quick, convenient visual reading of the sensor, saving them the time of checking the monitoring system's data. The Local Temperature Display: Logs and displays the highest and lowest temperatures Provides an audible alarm for out-of-range temperatures Offers a relay output that can be programmed to turn on a light or siren for additional onsite alarm notifications. When the temperature falls out of the programmed high and low limits, the display triggers an audible alarm, immediately alerting onsite staff of a potential problem. This alarm is in addition to the alerts that the Sensaphone monitoring system sends to designated personnel when temperatures fall out of range. The temperature sensor display is easy to program by using the four buttons on the front. It connects to the sensor via screw terminals on the bottom and wirelessly with the Sensaphone WSG30. The display comes with a battery that powers it for up to one year, but you can also use an optional external power supply. Name: Sensaphone Sentry Monitoring System
Description: A Cellular-Based Monitoring Solution…. No More Telephone Lines A supervised connection assures the Sentry is online and monitoring at all times Check sensor status or make programming changes with the Sensaphone mobile App or Sensaphone.net website. Notifies an unlimited number of people by e-mail, text message or voice phone calls in the event of an alarm Up to five external sensors monitor temperature, humidity, water and more LTE-M Cellular technology ensures device connectivity in difficult locations via AT&T, Verizon, and multiple Canadian carriers. Temperature sensing range of -109° to 115°F | -85° to 57°C Manage multiple units, make programming changes and access status conditions using a simple, powerful web-based interface Low cost subscription includes all online hosting, cellular connection fees, and unlimited text, phone and email alerts Contact us before purchasing to confirm cellular coverage Antenna and SIM card included Made in USA Available in optional weatherproof NEMA 4X enclosure The Sensaphone Sentry Monitoring System offers an easy-to-install, easy-to-configure monitoring solution for locations with no access to a landline or Internet connection. The system monitors up to five different environmental and equipment status conditions including temperature, humidity, power failure and water detection. When the system identifies issues, it instantly sends alerts via phone, text or email over a cellular connection. Users can access information and make system changes from any web-enabled device. No software is required, so installation, integration and management are easy. INCLUDED: Internal rechargeable battery-backup system will provide 12 hours of continuous monitoring and alerting in the event of a power outage. Name: Sensaphone WEB600 Battery Backup
Description: The WEB600 Battery Backup module (FGD-W610-B) provides uninterrupted backup power when main power fails. The module includes a rechargeable battery and charge control circuitry to keep the battery fully charged while connected to a powered WEB600. The module can provide up to 2 hours of backup time in the event of a power failure. The battery module connects to the WEB600 using a short cable included with the battery module. Simply connect the cable to the Battery Backup connector on the WEB600. The unit will charge the battery and monitor its charge level. The percent charge can be viewed on the WEB600's Summary screen.   Downloads Sensaphone WEB600 Battery Backup Addendum
